The aggregate principal amount of all Term Loans on the Closing Date shall be \$350,000,000. Amounts repaid or prepaid in respect of Term Loans may not be reborrowed. The Term Loan A Commitment of each Term Lender shall terminate immediately and without further action upon the funding of such Term Lender\'s Term Loan on the Closing Date.

**(b) Revolving Loans.** Subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, each Revolving Lender severally (and not jointly) agrees to make revolving loans (each, a \"**Revolving Loan**\") to the Borrower from time to time during the period from the Closing Date to and including the Business Day immediately preceding the Revolving Maturity Date, in an aggregate principal amount at any time outstanding not to exceed such Lender\'s Revolving Commitment *minus* such Lender\'s Pro Rata Share of the aggregate outstanding LC Exposure at such time. The aggregate amount of all Revolving Loans outstanding at any time, together with the aggregate outstanding LC Exposure, shall not exceed the aggregate Revolving Commitments then in effect (such aggregate amount, the \"**Total Revolving Exposure**\"). Within the foregoing limits, and subject to the terms, conditions, and limitations set forth herein, the Borrower may borrow, repay, and reborrow Revolving Loans. The aggregate Revolving Commitments on the Closing Date are \$125,000,000.

**[Section 2.02 --- Borrowings, Conversions, and Continuations]{.underline}**

**(a) Borrowing Requests.** Each borrowing of Loans shall be made upon the Borrower\'s irrevocable written notice to the Administrative Agent in the form of a Borrowing Request (a \"**Borrowing Request**\"), which Borrowing Request shall be substantially in the form of Exhibit C hereto (or such other form as may be approved by the Administrative Agent) and must be received by the Administrative Agent not later than (i) 12:00 noon (Eastern Time) three Business Days prior to the requested date of any borrowing of, or conversion to or continuation of, SOFR Loans and (ii) 12:00 noon (Eastern Time) one Business Day prior to the requested date of any borrowing of ABR Loans. Each Borrowing Request shall specify (A) whether the Borrower is requesting a Revolving Loan or Term Loan, (B) the aggregate amount of the requested borrowing, (C) the date of such borrowing (which shall be a Business Day), (D) whether such borrowing is to be a borrowing of SOFR Loans or ABR Loans, and (E) in the case of a borrowing of SOFR Loans, the initial Interest Period to be applicable thereto. If no election as to the Type of borrowing is specified, then the requested borrowing shall be an ABR Loan. If no Interest Period is specified with respect to any requested borrowing of SOFR Loans, then the Borrower shall be deemed to have selected an Interest Period of one month\'s duration.

**(b) Minimum Amounts.** Each borrowing of SOFR Loans shall be in a minimum principal amount of \$5,000,000 and in integral multiples of \$1,000,000 in excess thereof (or the remaining available amount of the applicable Commitment). Each borrowing of ABR Loans shall be in a minimum principal amount of \$1,000,000 and in integral multiples of \$500,000 in excess thereof (or the remaining available amount of the applicable Commitment).

**(c) Interest Period Elections.** The Borrower may elect Interest Periods of 1, 3, or 6 months\' duration (or, if agreed to by all affected Lenders, 12 months\' duration) with respect to SOFR Loans. Any such election shall be made by delivery of an Interest Election Request in accordance with Section 2.02(d).

**(d) Conversion and Continuation.** The Borrower may elect from time to time to convert SOFR Loans to ABR Loans by giving the Administrative Agent at least one Business Day\'s prior irrevocable written notice of such election, and the Borrower may elect from time to time to convert ABR Loans to SOFR Loans, or to continue SOFR Loans for an additional Interest Period, by giving the Administrative Agent at least three Business Days\' prior irrevocable written notice of such election, in each case by delivery of an Interest Election Request substantially in the form of Exhibit D hereto (or such other form as may be approved by the Administrative Agent). Each such Interest Election Request shall specify (i) the Loans to which such Interest Election Request applies and, if different options are being elected with respect to different portions thereof, the portions thereof to be allocated to each resulting borrowing (in which case the information to be specified pursuant to clauses (ii) through (iv) below shall be specified for each resulting borrowing), (ii) the effective date of the election made pursuant to such Interest Election Request (which shall be a Business Day), (iii) whether the resulting borrowing is to be a SOFR Loan or ABR Loan, and (iv) if the resulting borrowing is a SOFR Loan, the Interest Period to be applicable thereto. If any such Interest Election Request requests a SOFR Loan but does not specify an Interest Period, then the Borrower shall be deemed to have selected an Interest Period of one month\'s duration.

**(e) Administrative Agent Actions.** Promptly following receipt of a Borrowing Request or an Interest Election Request, the Administrative Agent shall advise each applicable Lender of the details thereof and of the amount of such Lender\'s Loan to be made or its participation in the relevant conversion or continuation.

**(f) Funding by Lenders.** Unless the Administrative Agent shall have received notice from a Lender prior to the proposed date of any borrowing that such Lender will not make available to the Administrative Agent such Lender\'s share of such borrowing, the Administrative Agent may assume that such Lender has made such share available on such date in accordance with this Section 2.02 and may, in reliance upon such assumption, make available to the Borrower a corresponding amount. In such event, if a Lender has not in fact made its share of the applicable borrowing available to the Administrative Agent, then the applicable Lender and the Borrower severally agree to pay to the Administrative Agent forthwith on demand such corresponding amount in immediately available funds with interest thereon, for each day from and including the date such amount is made available to the Borrower to but excluding the date of payment to the Administrative Agent, at (i) in the case of a payment to be made by such Lender, the greater of the Federal Funds Rate and a rate determined by the Administrative Agent in accordance with banking industry rules on interbank compensation, and (ii) in the case of a payment to be made by the Borrower, the interest rate applicable to ABR Loans.

**[Section 2.03 --- Incremental Facility]{.underline}**

**(a) Request for Incremental Facility.** At any time and from time to time after the Closing Date, subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Section 2.03, the Borrower may, by written notice to the Administrative Agent, request one or more increases in the aggregate amount of the existing Revolving Commitments or one or more tranches of additional term loan commitments (each, an \"**Incremental Facility**\"), in an aggregate principal amount not to exceed \$75,000,000 (the \"**Incremental Cap**\"); *provided* that (i) the Borrower shall be in compliance with the financial covenants set forth in Section 6.10 on a Pro Forma Basis after giving effect to the full drawing of any Incremental Facility (in the case of an increase in Revolving Commitments) or the incurrence of the full amount of any additional term loans, (ii) no Default or Event of Default has occurred and is continuing or would result from such incurrence, (iii) the representations and warranties set forth in Article IV are true and correct in all material respects (or, if qualified by materiality or Material Adverse Effect, in all respects) on and as of the date of the incurrence of such Incremental Facility (except to the extent such representations and warranties expressly relate to an earlier date, in which case as of such earlier date), and (iv) the Borrower shall have delivered to the Administrative Agent a certificate of a Financial Officer demonstrating compliance with the foregoing conditions.

**(b) Terms of Incremental Facility.** Each Incremental Facility shall (i) rank *pari passu* in right of payment and of security with the existing Facilities, (ii) not have a maturity date earlier than the Latest First Lien Maturity Date at the time of incurrence (in the case of term loans), (iii) have a weighted average life to maturity no shorter than the remaining weighted average life to maturity of the existing Term Loans at the time of incurrence (in the case of term loans), (iv) be on terms and conditions (other than pricing, interest rate margins, rate floors, fees, and optional prepayment and redemption terms) substantially identical to, or (taken as a whole) no more favorable to the lenders thereunder than, the terms of the existing Facilities (except for covenants or other provisions applicable only to periods after the Latest First Lien Maturity Date at the time of incurrence), as determined by the Administrative Agent in its reasonable discretion, and (v) be subject to MFN pricing protections: if the effective yield (including upfront fees and original issue discount but excluding any arrangement, structuring, or similar fees payable in connection therewith that are not shared with all lenders of such Incremental Facility on a pro rata basis) applicable to any incremental term loan is greater than the effective yield applicable to the existing Term Loans by more than 0.50% per annum, the Applicable Rate with respect to the existing Term Loans shall be increased to the extent necessary so that the effective yield applicable to the existing Term Loans is equal to the effective yield applicable to such incremental term loan *minus* 0.50% per annum. The Administrative Agent shall consent (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, delayed, or conditioned) to each Incremental Facility.

**(c) Incremental Facility as First Lien Obligations.** For the avoidance of doubt, any Incremental Facility shall constitute \"First Lien Obligations\" for purposes of this Agreement and the Intercreditor Agreement, and the Liens securing any such Incremental Facility shall constitute first-priority Liens on the Collateral *pari passu* with the Liens securing the existing Obligations.

**(d) Amendments.** This Agreement and the other Loan Documents may be amended in connection with any Incremental Facility to the extent necessary to reflect such Incremental Facility, including to provide for the pro rata sharing of mandatory prepayments and other similar provisions, without the consent of any existing Lender.

**[Section 2.04 --- Letters of Credit]{.underline}**

**(a) General.** Subject to the terms and conditions set forth herein, the Issuing Bank agrees to issue, at the request of the Borrower, Letters of Credit for the account of the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary from time to time during the period from the Closing Date to and including the date that is five Business Days prior to the Revolving Maturity Date in such form as may be approved from time to time by the Issuing Bank. The Letter of Credit Sub-Facility is a sub-facility of the Revolving Credit Facility, and Letters of Credit issued hereunder shall reduce the amount available for Revolving Loans on a dollar-for-dollar basis. The aggregate face amount of all outstanding Letters of Credit shall not at any time exceed \$25,000,000. No Letter of Credit shall be issued if, after giving effect thereto, (i) the aggregate LC Exposure would exceed \$25,000,000 or (ii) the Total Revolving Exposure would exceed the aggregate Revolving Commitments then in effect.

**(b) Issuance, Amendment, Renewal, Extension.** Each Letter of Credit shall be issued, amended, renewed, or extended upon not less than three Business Days\' prior irrevocable written notice (or such shorter period as is acceptable to the Issuing Bank) from the Borrower to the Issuing Bank (with a copy to the Administrative Agent), specifying the date of issuance, amendment, renewal, or extension (which shall be a Business Day), the date on which such Letter of Credit is to expire (which shall comply with clause (c) below), the amount of such Letter of Credit, the name and address of the beneficiary thereof, and such other information as shall be necessary to prepare, amend, or extend such Letter of Credit. Each Letter of Credit shall expire no later than the earlier of (i) the date that is one year after the date of the issuance of such Letter of Credit (or, in the case of any renewal or extension thereof, one year after such renewal or extension) and (ii) the date that is five Business Days prior to the Revolving Maturity Date; *provided* that any Letter of Credit with a one-year expiry date may provide for the renewal thereof for additional one-year periods (which shall in no event extend beyond the date referred to in clause (ii) above) unless the Issuing Bank notifies the beneficiary thereof at least 30 days prior to the then-applicable expiry date that such Letter of Credit will not be renewed.

**(c) Participations.** By the issuance of a Letter of Credit (or an amendment to a Letter of Credit increasing the amount thereof) and without any further action on the part of the Issuing Bank or the Revolving Lenders, the Issuing Bank hereby grants to each Revolving Lender, and each Revolving Lender hereby irrevocably and unconditionally acquires from the Issuing Bank, without recourse or warranty, an undivided interest and participation, to the extent of such Revolving Lender\'s Pro Rata Share, in such Letter of Credit, each drawing made thereunder, and the obligations of the Borrower arising in connection therewith. Each Revolving Lender acknowledges and agrees that its obligation to acquire participations pursuant to this Section 2.04(c) in respect of Letters of Credit is absolute and unconditional and shall not be affected by any circumstance whatsoever, including any amendment, renewal, or extension of any Letter of Credit or the occurrence and continuance of a Default or Event of Default or reduction or termination of the Revolving Commitments.

**(d) Reimbursement.** If the Issuing Bank shall make any LC Disbursement in respect of a Letter of Credit, the Borrower shall reimburse the Issuing Bank in an amount equal to such LC Disbursement not later than 12:00 noon (Eastern Time) on the Business Day immediately following the date on which the Borrower receives notice from the Issuing Bank or the Administrative Agent of such LC Disbursement, if the Borrower shall have received such notice on or prior to 10:00 a.m. (Eastern Time) on such day, or, if the Borrower shall have received such notice after 10:00 a.m. (Eastern Time) on such day, not later than 12:00 noon (Eastern Time) on the second Business Day following such date. If the Borrower fails to make such payment when due, the Administrative Agent shall notify each Revolving Lender of the applicable LC Disbursement, the payment then due from the Borrower in respect thereof, and such Revolving Lender\'s Pro Rata Share thereof. Promptly following receipt of such notice, each Revolving Lender shall pay to the Administrative Agent its Pro Rata Share of such LC Disbursement, and the amount so paid shall be deemed to constitute an ABR Revolving Loan made by such Revolving Lender (and the aggregate amount so deemed made shall reduce the Borrower\'s reimbursement obligation accordingly). LC Exposure shall reduce the available Revolving Commitments at all times.

**(e) Cash Collateralization.** If an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing, or as otherwise required under Section 2.05 or Section 7.01, the Borrower shall, promptly upon demand by the Administrative Agent, deposit in an account with the Administrative Agent, in the name of the Administrative Agent and for the benefit of the Revolving Lenders and the Issuing Bank, an amount in cash equal to 103% of the aggregate LC Exposure as of such date (the \"**LC Cash Collateral Amount**\"), to be held as cash collateral for the repayment of the Borrower\'s obligations in respect of such outstanding Letters of Credit. The Borrower hereby grants to the Administrative Agent, for the benefit of the Issuing Bank and the Revolving Lenders, a first-priority security interest in all such cash, deposit accounts, and all balances therein and all proceeds of the foregoing. Cash collateral held pursuant to this Section 2.04(e) shall be released promptly upon (i) the elimination of the applicable LC Exposure (by expiry, cancellation, drawing, or cash collateralization in another manner satisfactory to the Issuing Bank) or (ii) the Administrative Agent\'s good faith determination that there exists excess cash collateral, in each case, so long as no Event of Default has occurred and is continuing. Notwithstanding the foregoing, cash collateral held with respect to any Letter of Credit shall not be released so long as the Issuing Bank has not been furnished with a replacement letter of credit satisfactory to it, if applicable.

**(f) LC Exposure in Purchase Context.** For any purchase of First Lien Obligations under the Intercreditor Agreement\'s Second Lien Purchase Right (or any similar right granted thereunder), the purchaser must, simultaneously with the consummation of such purchase, either (i) deposit cash collateral in an amount equal to 103% of the aggregate face amount of all outstanding Letters of Credit in an account with the Administrative Agent (or its successor) for the benefit of the Issuing Bank, or (ii) deliver to the Issuing Bank one or more back-to-back replacement letters of credit, in form and substance satisfactory to the Issuing Bank, in replacement of each outstanding Letter of Credit. Outstanding LC Exposure shall be included in the calculation of the Purchase Price payable by any such purchaser under the terms of the Intercreditor Agreement. The Administrative Agent shall cooperate in good faith with the Second Lien Agent and the applicable purchaser to effectuate any such purchase in accordance with the Intercreditor Agreement, including the transfer of the Register, the assignment of Commitments and Loans, and the cash collateralization or replacement of Letters of Credit contemplated by this Section 2.04(f).

**[Section 2.05 --- Mandatory Prepayments]{.underline}**

**(a) Term Loan Amortization.** The Borrower shall repay the aggregate outstanding principal amount of the Term Loans in consecutive quarterly installments on the last Business Day of each fiscal quarter (commencing with the first full fiscal quarter ending after the Closing Date), each in an amount equal to 1.25% of the original aggregate principal amount of the Term Loans (i.e., \$4,375,000 per quarter), with the remaining outstanding principal balance of the Term Loans, together with all accrued and unpaid interest thereon, due and payable on the Term Loan Maturity Date. Each such installment shall be applied to the outstanding Term Loans of each Term Lender on a pro rata basis.

**(b) Asset Dispositions and Casualty Events.**

> **(i)** Subject to Section 2.05(b)(iv), upon the consummation of any Asset Disposition or the receipt of any insurance proceeds or condemnation awards in respect of any Casualty Event, in each case where the Net Cash Proceeds therefrom exceed \$5,000,000 individually, or where the aggregate Net Cash Proceeds from all Asset Dispositions and Casualty Events during any fiscal year of the Borrower exceed \$15,000,000 in the aggregate, the Borrower shall, within five Business Days of the receipt of such Net Cash Proceeds (or, if applicable, upon expiration of the Reinvestment Period as provided in Section 2.05(b)(iv)), prepay the Term Loans in an amount equal to 100% of such Net Cash Proceeds (net of any amounts permitted to be reinvested under Section 2.05(b)(iv)).
>
> **(ii)** *Application.* All mandatory prepayments required under this Section 2.05(b) shall be applied as follows: *first*, to the outstanding principal amount of the Term Loans on a pro rata basis among all Term Lenders (applied to the remaining scheduled amortization payments in direct order of maturity, unless the Required Lenders otherwise direct), and *second*, to cash collateralize any outstanding LC Exposure at 103% of the aggregate face amount thereof. For purposes of the Intercreditor Agreement, all such mandatory prepayments shall constitute payments on account of First Lien Obligations and shall be applied prior and senior to any application on account of Second Lien Obligations. No Revolving Commitment shall be permanently reduced as a result of any mandatory prepayment under this Section 2.05(b).
>
> **(iii)** *Anti-Evasion.* No Asset Disposition (and no series of related Asset Dispositions) shall be structured, timed, or otherwise arranged in a manner designed to avoid the \$5,000,000 individual threshold or the \$15,000,000 annual aggregate threshold set forth in this Section 2.05(b). The Administrative Agent may, in its reasonable discretion, aggregate any Asset Dispositions occurring within a 90-day period if the Administrative Agent reasonably determines that such Asset Dispositions constitute a single plan or related series of transactions, and, in such event, the Net Cash Proceeds from all such aggregated Asset Dispositions shall be treated as Net Cash Proceeds from a single Asset Disposition for purposes of determining whether the \$5,000,000 individual threshold has been exceeded. In making such determination, the Administrative Agent shall consider, among other factors, the identity of the acquirer or acquirers, the nature and location of the assets disposed of, and the timing and sequencing of the dispositions.
>
> **(iv)** *Reinvestment Rights.* Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions of this Section 2.05(b), the Borrower may elect, by delivery of a Reinvestment Notice to the Administrative Agent within 10 Business Days of receipt of any Net Cash Proceeds subject to the mandatory prepayment provisions of Section 2.05(b)(i), to reinvest all or a portion of such Net Cash Proceeds in Permitted Reinvestment Assets within the Reinvestment Period (i.e., the 365-day period following the date of receipt of such Net Cash Proceeds). The Reinvestment Notice shall specify the amount of Net Cash Proceeds to be reinvested and a general description of the Permitted Reinvestment Assets to be acquired or the capital expenditures to be made. To the extent the Borrower does not so reinvest (or commit to reinvest pursuant to a binding agreement entered into during the Reinvestment Period) such Net Cash Proceeds (or the applicable portion thereof) in Permitted Reinvestment Assets within the Reinvestment Period, the Borrower shall make a mandatory prepayment of the Term Loans in an amount equal to the unreinvested portion of such Net Cash Proceeds within five Business Days after the expiration of the Reinvestment Period, such prepayment to constitute a 100% sweep of non-reinvested Net Cash Proceeds. For the avoidance of doubt, the reinvestment rights under this Section 2.05(b)(iv) are subject to the provisions of the Intercreditor Agreement, including Section 4.02 thereof, governing the application of asset disposition and casualty proceeds as between First Lien Obligations and Second Lien Obligations, and no reinvestment of Net Cash Proceeds shall be permitted to the extent it would violate the terms of the Intercreditor Agreement.
>
> **(v)** *Prepayment Event Notice / Reporting Threshold.* The Borrower shall deliver to the Administrative Agent a written notice substantially in the form of Exhibit E hereto (a \"**Prepayment Event Notice**\") not later than five Business Days after any Asset Disposition or Casualty Event yielding Net Cash Proceeds (whether estimated or actual) in excess of \$3,000,000 (the \"**Reporting Threshold**\"). The Reporting Threshold of \$3,000,000 is set below the \$5,000,000 individual prepayment trigger to provide the Administrative Agent and the Lenders with early visibility into potential mandatory prepayment events and to enable the Administrative Agent to monitor compliance with the anti-evasion provisions of Section 2.05(b)(iii). Each Prepayment Event Notice shall describe in reasonable detail (A) the applicable Asset Disposition or Casualty Event, including the assets involved and the identity of the purchaser or the nature of the casualty, (B) the estimated Net Cash Proceeds therefrom, (C) the expected date of receipt of such Net Cash Proceeds, and (D) whether the Borrower intends to deliver a Reinvestment Notice pursuant to Section 2.05(b)(iv) with respect to all or any portion of such Net Cash Proceeds. The Administrative Agent shall promptly distribute copies of each Prepayment Event Notice to the Lenders.

**(c) Excess Cash Flow Sweep.** Within 10 Business Days after the date on which audited annual financial statements are delivered (or required to be delivered) pursuant to Section 5.01(a), commencing with the fiscal year ending December 31, 2025, the Borrower shall prepay the outstanding Term Loans in an amount equal to the applicable percentage (set forth below) of Excess Cash Flow for the fiscal year then ended *minus* (without duplication) the aggregate amount of all voluntary prepayments of Term Loans and permanent Revolving Commitment reductions made during such fiscal year (or, at the Borrower\'s election, during the period from the end of such fiscal year through the date that is five Business Days prior to the date the related Excess Cash Flow prepayment is due):

> \(i\) 75% of Excess Cash Flow if the Total First Lien Net Leverage Ratio as of the last day of such fiscal year is greater than 4.50:1.00;
>
> \(ii\) 50% of Excess Cash Flow if the Total First Lien Net Leverage Ratio as of the last day of such fiscal year is less than or equal to 4.50:1.00 but greater than 3.50:1.00;
>
> \(iii\) 25% of Excess Cash Flow if the Total First Lien Net Leverage Ratio as of the last day of such fiscal year is less than or equal to 3.50:1.00 but greater than 2.50:1.00; and
>
> \(iv\) 0% of Excess Cash Flow if the Total First Lien Net Leverage Ratio as of the last day of such fiscal year is less than or equal to 2.50:1.00.

\"**Excess Cash Flow**\" means, for any period, Consolidated EBITDA for such period *minus* the sum of (A) Capital Expenditures made in cash during such period (other than Capital Expenditures financed with the proceeds of Indebtedness (other than Revolving Loans)), (B) scheduled principal payments of Indebtedness (including scheduled amortization of the Term Loans, but excluding mandatory prepayments under this Section 2.05) made during such period, (C) cash taxes paid during such period, (D) cash interest expense paid during such period (excluding interest paid with proceeds of Indebtedness), (E) the aggregate amount of any Permitted Acquisitions consummated during such period funded with internally generated cash flow, (F) cash restructuring charges paid during such period, and (G) changes in working capital for such period.

All Excess Cash Flow prepayments shall be applied to the outstanding Term Loans on a pro rata basis among all Term Lenders in direct order of maturity.

**(d) Debt Issuance Proceeds.** The Borrower shall prepay the outstanding Term Loans in an amount equal to 100% of the net cash proceeds received by the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary from any issuance or incurrence of any Indebtedness not permitted under Article VI (including Section 6.01), promptly (and in any event within five Business Days) following the receipt of such net cash proceeds. Such prepayment shall be applied to the outstanding Term Loans on a pro rata basis among all Term Lenders.

**(e) Application and Pro Rata Sharing.** All mandatory prepayments under this Section 2.05 shall be applied to the outstanding Term Loans on a pro rata basis among all Term Lenders (and, after the Term Loans have been repaid in full, to the cash collateralization of outstanding LC Exposure and then to the permanent reduction of Revolving Commitments, in each case only upon a Change of Control or Event of Default). Mandatory prepayments shall be applied in direct order of maturity to the remaining scheduled amortization payments, unless the Required Lenders otherwise direct by written notice to the Administrative Agent delivered within five Business Days of receipt of notice of such mandatory prepayment. Revolving Commitments shall not be permanently reduced by any mandatory prepayment under this Section 2.05, except as expressly provided above. Each mandatory prepayment under this Section 2.05 shall be accompanied by any accrued and unpaid interest on the principal amount prepaid through the date of prepayment and any breakage costs payable under Section 2.13.

**[Section 2.06 --- Optional Prepayments]{.underline}**

**(a)** The Borrower may, upon irrevocable written notice to the Administrative Agent, at any time and from time to time prepay the Term Loans and Revolving Loans, in whole or in part, without penalty or premium, subject to (i) prior notice of at least three Business Days for SOFR Loans and one Business Day for ABR Loans, (ii) minimum prepayment amounts of \$5,000,000 and integral multiples of \$1,000,000 in excess thereof (or, if less, the entire outstanding principal amount thereof), and (iii) payment of any breakage costs payable under Section 2.13.

**(b)** Each such notice shall specify the date and amount of such prepayment and the Loans to be prepaid and whether such Loans are SOFR Loans or ABR Loans (and, in the case of SOFR Loans, the Interest Period applicable thereto). Promptly following receipt of any such notice relating to Term Loans, the Administrative Agent shall advise the applicable Lenders of the contents thereof. Each partial prepayment of Term Loans shall be applied to the remaining scheduled amortization payments of the Term Loans in direct order of maturity (or, at the Borrower\'s election, ratably), unless the Required Lenders otherwise direct. Each partial prepayment of Revolving Loans shall be applied to the Revolving Loans of each Revolving Lender on a pro rata basis.

**(c)** No repricing protection or prepayment premium shall be required for any optional prepayment of Term Loans or Revolving Loans at any time.

**[Section 2.07 --- Commitment Reductions]{.underline}**

**(a)** The Borrower may, upon at least three Business Days\' prior irrevocable written notice to the Administrative Agent, terminate in whole or permanently reduce in part the unused portion of the Revolving Commitments; *provided* that (i) any such partial reduction shall be in a minimum principal amount of \$5,000,000 and in integral multiples of \$1,000,000 in excess thereof, and (ii) the Borrower shall not terminate or reduce the Revolving Commitments if, after giving effect to any concurrent prepayment of Revolving Loans and cash collateralization of LC Exposure, the Total Revolving Exposure would exceed the aggregate Revolving Commitments then in effect. Each reduction of Revolving Commitments shall be applied to each Revolving Lender\'s Revolving Commitment on a pro rata basis.

**(b)** All Revolving Commitments shall automatically and permanently terminate on the Revolving Maturity Date.

**[Section 2.08 --- Interest]{.underline}**

**(a) Non-Default Rate.** Each Loan shall bear interest on the outstanding principal amount thereof from the applicable borrowing date at a rate per annum equal to:

> \(i\) in the case of SOFR Loans, Adjusted Term SOFR for the applicable Interest Period *plus* the Applicable Rate (which, at Pricing Level I, is 2.50% per annum, or 250 basis points); or
>
> \(ii\) in the case of ABR Loans, the ABR *plus* the Applicable Rate (which, at Pricing Level I, is 1.50% per annum, or 150 basis points).

For illustrative purposes only, as of June 27, 2025, the Term SOFR Reference Rate for a one-month Interest Period was approximately 5.33% per annum, which, together with the SOFR Adjustment of 0.10% and the Applicable Rate of 2.50% (at Pricing Level I), yields an all-in non-default interest rate for one-month SOFR Loans of approximately 7.93% per annum.

**(b) Default Rate.** Upon the occurrence and during the continuance of any Event of Default, the Borrower shall pay interest on all outstanding Obligations (including, without limitation, all outstanding principal, interest, fees, and other amounts) at a rate per annum equal to the rate otherwise applicable thereto pursuant to Section 2.08(a) *plus* 2.00% (200 basis points) (the \"**Default Rate**\"). For the avoidance of doubt, the Default Rate for SOFR Loans at Pricing Level I would be Adjusted Term SOFR plus 4.50% (450 basis points), and the Default Rate for ABR Loans at Pricing Level I would be ABR plus 3.50% (350 basis points). Default interest shall accrue from the date of such Event of Default and shall be payable on demand. For purposes of the Intercreditor Agreement, the distinction between the non-default contract rate and the Default Rate is relevant to the calculation of the Purchase Price under any Second Lien Purchase Right, and any calculation of First Lien Obligations for purposes of such Purchase Price shall include accrued and unpaid Default Rate interest.

**(c) Interest Periods.** The Borrower may select Interest Periods of 1, 3, or 6 months for SOFR Loans, or, if all affected Lenders agree, Interest Periods of 12 months. The Borrower may not select an Interest Period that would extend beyond the applicable Maturity Date. Each Interest Period for SOFR Loans shall commence on the date of the applicable borrowing or on the last day of the immediately preceding Interest Period applicable thereto, as the case may be.

**(d) Payment.** Interest on each SOFR Loan shall be payable in arrears on the last day of each Interest Period applicable thereto and, in the case of SOFR Loans with an Interest Period exceeding three months, also on each day that occurs at three-month intervals after the first day of such Interest Period. Interest on each ABR Loan shall be payable in arrears on the last Business Day of each March, June, September, and December of each year, commencing with the first such date after the Closing Date. Interest on all Loans shall also be payable on the applicable Maturity Date and, after acceleration, on demand. Default interest payable under Section 2.08(b) shall be payable on demand.

**[Section 2.09 --- Fees]{.underline}**

**(a) Commitment Fee.** The Borrower shall pay to the Administrative Agent, for the pro rata account of each Revolving Lender in accordance with its Pro Rata Share, a commitment fee (the \"**Commitment Fee**\") at a rate per annum equal to the Commitment Fee Rate set forth in the definition of \"Applicable Rate\" (initially, 0.375% per annum) on the average daily unused portion of each such Revolving Lender\'s Revolving Commitment during the period from the Closing Date to the Revolving Maturity Date. For purposes of computing the Commitment Fee, outstanding Revolving Loans and the aggregate amount of LC Exposure shall be deemed to be a use of the Revolving Commitments. Commitment Fees shall accrue from the Closing Date and shall be payable quarterly in arrears on the last Business Day of each March, June, September, and December of each year, commencing with the first such date after the Closing Date, and on the Revolving Maturity Date.

**(b) LC Fees.** The Borrower shall pay (i) to the Administrative Agent, for the pro rata account of each Revolving Lender in accordance with its Pro Rata Share, a participation fee (the \"**LC Participation Fee**\") at a rate per annum equal to the Applicable Rate for SOFR Revolving Loans (initially, 2.50% per annum) on the average daily stated amount of each outstanding Letter of Credit during the period from the date of issuance of such Letter of Credit to the date such Letter of Credit expires, is terminated, or is fully drawn, payable quarterly in arrears on the last Business Day of each March, June, September, and December of each year, commencing with the first such date after the issuance of such Letter of Credit, and on the date such Letter of Credit expires, is terminated, or is fully drawn, and (ii) to the Issuing Bank directly, a fronting fee at a rate per annum equal to 0.125% (12.5 basis points) on the stated amount of each outstanding Letter of Credit, payable quarterly in arrears, and such Issuing Bank\'s customary issuance, amendment, negotiation, transfer, drawing, and other charges and fees.

**(c) Administrative Agent Fee.** The Borrower shall pay to the Administrative Agent, for its own account, an annual administrative agent fee as separately agreed upon between the Borrower and the Administrative Agent in the Fee Letter. Such fee shall be payable in advance on the Closing Date and on each anniversary thereof.

**[Section 2.10 --- Computation of Interest and Fees]{.underline}**

**(a)** All computations of interest for SOFR Loans and computations of the Commitment Fee and LC Participation Fee shall be made on the basis of a year of 360 days and actual days elapsed (including the first day but excluding the last day) occurring in the period for which such interest or fee is payable. All computations of interest for ABR Loans (to the extent based on the Prime Rate or the Federal Funds Rate) shall be made on the basis of a year of 365 days (or 366 days in a leap year) and actual days elapsed (including the first day but excluding the last day); *provided* that the component of the ABR based on Adjusted Term SOFR shall be computed on the basis of a year of 360 days.

**(b)** Each determination of an interest rate by the Administrative Agent pursuant to any provision of this Agreement shall be conclusive and binding on the Borrower and the Lenders in the absence of manifest error.

**(c)** If any applicable interest rate (including the ABR and Adjusted Term SOFR) would, at any time and for any reason, be less than the applicable floor set forth in the definitions thereof, such interest rate shall be deemed to be equal to such floor.

**[Section 2.11 --- Evidence of Indebtedness]{.underline}**

**(a)** The Administrative Agent shall maintain at its Principal Office a register (the \"**Register**\") in which it shall record (i) the name and address of each Lender and the amount of each Lender\'s Commitment and the principal amount and stated interest of each Loan made by such Lender, (ii) the amount of any sum received by the Administrative Agent hereunder from or for the account of the Borrower, and (iii) the amount of any sum received by the Administrative Agent hereunder for the account of each Lender and each Lender\'s share thereof. The entries in the Register shall be conclusive and binding for all purposes, absent manifest error. The Register shall be available for inspection by the Borrower and any Lender at any reasonable time and from time to time upon reasonable prior notice.

**(b)** Each Lender shall maintain its own records of the Loans made by it and the payments of principal and interest thereon. In the event of any conflict between the entries in the Register and any Lender\'s records, the entries in the Register shall govern, absent manifest error .

**(c)** Any failure of the Administrative Agent or any Lender to maintain the Register or any such records, or any error therein, shall not in any manner affect the obligation of the Borrower to repay the Loans and other Obligations in accordance with their terms. At the request of any Lender, the Borrower shall execute and deliver to such Lender a promissory note in substantially the form of Exhibit G evidencing such Lender\'s Loans.

**[Section 2.12 --- Payments Generally]{.underline}**

**(a)** The Borrower shall make each payment required to be made by it hereunder or under any other Loan Document (whether of principal, interest, fees, reimbursement of LC Disbursements, or amounts payable under Section 2.13, or otherwise) prior to 2:00 p.m. (Eastern Time) on the date when due, in immediately available funds, free and clear of any setoff, recoupment, counterclaim, or other defense, to the Administrative Agent at the Principal Office or such other office as the Administrative Agent may designate in writing from time to time. Any amounts received after such time on any date may, in the discretion of the Administrative Agent, be deemed to have been received on the next succeeding Business Day for purposes of calculating interest thereon.

**(b)** All such payments shall be applied to the Obligations then due and owing in the manner required by the Loan Documents. If the due date of any payment under this Agreement would otherwise fall on a day that is not a Business Day, such due date shall be extended to the next succeeding Business Day, and interest shall accrue and be payable for the period of such extension; *provided* that in the case of any SOFR Loan, if such next succeeding Business Day would fall in the next calendar month, such due date shall instead be the immediately preceding Business Day.

**(c)** The Administrative Agent shall distribute to each Lender promptly after its receipt of each payment from the Borrower, such Lender\'s applicable share (if any) of such payment in like funds as received by wire transfer to such Lender\'s lending office or other account designated in writing by such Lender to the Administrative Agent. If the Administrative Agent receives funds for the account of the Lenders prior to 2:00 p.m. (Eastern Time), the Administrative Agent shall use commercially reasonable efforts to distribute such funds to the Lenders on the same Business Day.

**(d)** If any Lender shall, by exercising any right of setoff, banker\'s lien, or counterclaim or otherwise, obtain payment in respect of any principal of or interest on any of its Loans or participations in LC Disbursements resulting in such Lender receiving payment of a greater proportion of the aggregate amount of its Loans and participations in LC Disbursements and accrued interest thereon than the proportion received by any other similarly situated Lender, then the Lender receiving such greater proportion shall purchase (for cash at face value) participations in the Loans and participations in LC Disbursements of other Lenders to the extent necessary so that the benefit of all such payments shall be shared by the Lenders ratably in accordance with the aggregate amount of principal of and accrued interest on their respective Loans and participations in LC Disbursements; *provided* that (i) if any such participations are purchased and all or any portion of the payment giving rise thereto is recovered, such participations shall be rescinded and the purchase price restored to the extent of such recovery, without interest, and (ii) the provisions of this Section 2.12(d) shall not be construed to apply to any payment made by the Borrower pursuant to and in accordance with the express terms of this Agreement or any payment obtained by a Lender as consideration for the assignment of, or sale of, a participation in any of its Loans or participations in LC Disbursements to any assignee or participant.

**(e)** The Borrower agrees that, to the fullest extent permitted by Applicable Law, any Lender may exercise rights of setoff and counterclaim with respect to any deposit accounts or other obligations at any time owing by such Lender to or for the credit or the account of the Borrower against any of and all Obligations of the Borrower now or hereafter existing under this Agreement or any other Loan Document held by such Lender, irrespective of whether or not such Lender shall have made any demand under this Agreement or any other Loan Document and although such obligations may be unmatured. Each Lender agrees promptly to notify the Borrower and the Administrative Agent after any such setoff or counterclaim; *provided* that the failure to give such notice shall not affect the validity of such setoff or counterclaim.

**(f)** Unless the Administrative Agent shall have received notice from the Borrower prior to the date on which any payment is due to the Administrative Agent for the account of the Lenders or the Issuing Bank hereunder that the Borrower will not make such payment, the Administrative Agent may assume that the Borrower has made such payment on such date in accordance herewith and may, in reliance upon such assumption, distribute to the Lenders or the Issuing Bank, as the case may be, the amount due. In such event, if the Borrower has not in fact made such payment, then each of the Lenders or the Issuing Bank, as the case may be, severally agrees to repay to the Administrative Agent forthwith on demand the amount so distributed to such Lender or the Issuing Bank with interest thereon, for each day from and including the date such amount is distributed to it to but excluding the date of payment to the Administrative Agent, at the greater of the Federal Funds Rate and a rate determined by the Administrative Agent in accordance with banking industry rules on interbank compensation.

**(g)** For purposes of this Agreement, the term \"**Defaulting Lender**\" means any Lender that (i) has failed, within two Business Days of the date required to be funded or paid, to fund any portion of its Loans, participations in Letters of Credit, or other amounts required to be paid by it hereunder, unless such Lender notifies the Administrative Agent and the Borrower in writing that such failure is the result of such Lender\'s determination that one or more conditions precedent to funding has not been satisfied, (ii) has notified the Borrower, the Administrative Agent, or the Issuing Bank in writing that it does not intend to comply with any of its funding obligations hereunder or has made a public statement to that effect, (iii) has failed, within three Business Days after request by the Administrative Agent or the Borrower, to confirm in writing that it will comply with its prospective funding obligations hereunder, or (iv) has become the subject of a Bankruptcy Event or a bail-in action. Any determination by the Administrative Agent that a Lender is a Defaulting Lender under any one or more of clauses (i) through (iv) above shall be conclusive and binding absent manifest error, and such Lender shall be deemed to be a Defaulting Lender upon delivery of written notice of such determination to the Borrower, the Issuing Bank, and each Lender.

**(h)** Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, if any Lender becomes a Defaulting Lender, then, so long as such Lender is a Defaulting Lender:

> \(i\) to the extent permitted by Applicable Law, such Defaulting Lender\'s right to approve or disapprove any amendment, waiver, or consent with respect to this Agreement shall be restricted as provided in the definition of Required Lenders and Section 10.02;
>
> \(ii\) any payment of principal, interest, fees, or other amounts received by the Administrative Agent for the account of such Defaulting Lender (whether voluntary or mandatory, at maturity, pursuant to Article VII, or otherwise) or received by the Administrative Agent from a Defaulting Lender pursuant to Section 2.12(f) shall be applied at such time or times as may be determined by the Administrative Agent as follows: *first*, to the payment of any amounts owing by such Defaulting Lender to the Administrative Agent hereunder; *second*, to the payment on a pro rata basis of any amounts owing by such Defaulting Lender to the Issuing Bank hereunder; *third*, if so determined by the Administrative Agent or requested by the Issuing Bank, to be held as cash collateral for future funding obligations of the Defaulting Lender in respect of any participation in any Letter of Credit; *fourth*, as the Borrower may request (so long as no Default or Event of Default has occurred and is continuing), to the funding of any Loan or LC participation in respect of which such Defaulting Lender has failed to fund its portion thereof; *fifth*, if so directed by the Borrower and the Administrative Agent, to be applied to prepay the Loans of such Defaulting Lender; and *sixth*, to such Defaulting Lender or as otherwise directed by a court of competent jurisdiction; and
>
> \(iii\) the Commitments and outstanding Loans of such Defaulting Lender shall be excluded for purposes of calculating the commitment fee payable to such Defaulting Lender, and no commitment fee shall accrue on the unused portion of the Revolving Commitment of such Defaulting Lender during the period it is a Defaulting Lender.

**[Section 2.13 --- Taxes; Yield Protection]{.underline}**

**(a) Payments Free of Taxes.** Any and all payments by or on account of any obligation of the Borrower under any Loan Document shall be made free and clear of and without deduction or withholding for any Taxes, except as required by Applicable Law. If the Borrower or any other applicable withholding agent shall be required by Applicable Law to deduct or withhold any Taxes from such payments, then (i) if such Taxes are Indemnified Taxes, the sum payable shall be increased as necessary so that, after making all required deductions or withholdings (including deductions or withholdings applicable to additional sums payable under this Section 2.13), the Administrative Agent or the applicable Lender, as the case may be, receives an amount equal to the sum it would have received had no such deduction or withholding been made, (ii) the Borrower or such withholding agent shall make such deduction or withholding, and (iii) the Borrower or such withholding agent shall timely pay the full amount deducted or withheld to the relevant Governmental Authority in accordance with Applicable Law.

**(b) Payment of Other Taxes.** The Borrower shall timely pay to the relevant Governmental Authority in accordance with Applicable Law, or at the option of the Administrative Agent timely reimburse it for the payment of, any present or future stamp, court, documentary, intangible, filing, recording, excise, or property Taxes or similar Taxes arising from any payment made under any Loan Document or from the execution, delivery, performance, enforcement, or registration of, from the receipt or perfection of a security interest under, or otherwise with respect to, any Loan Document, except any such Taxes that are Other Connection Taxes imposed with respect to an assignment.

**(c) Indemnification by the Borrower.** The Borrower shall indemnify the Administrative Agent and each Lender, within 10 days after written demand therefor, for the full amount of any Indemnified Taxes or Other Taxes (including Indemnified Taxes or Other Taxes imposed or asserted on or attributable to amounts payable under this Section 2.13) payable or paid by such Administrative Agent or Lender or required to be withheld or deducted from a payment to such Administrative Agent or Lender, and any reasonable expenses arising therefrom or with respect thereto, whether or not such Indemnified Taxes or Other Taxes were correctly or legally imposed or asserted by the relevant Governmental Authority. A certificate setting forth in reasonable detail the basis and amount of such payment or liability delivered to the Borrower by a Lender or by the Administrative Agent on its own behalf or on behalf of a Lender shall be conclusive absent manifest error.

**(d) Evidence of Payments.** As soon as practicable after any payment of Taxes by the Borrower or any other applicable withholding agent to a Governmental Authority pursuant to this Section 2.13, the Borrower shall deliver to the Administrative Agent the original or a certified copy of a receipt issued by such Governmental Authority evidencing such payment, a copy of any return reporting such payment, or other evidence of such payment reasonably satisfactory to the Administrative Agent.

**(e) Indemnification by Lenders.** Each Lender shall severally indemnify the Administrative Agent, within 10 days after demand therefor, for (i) any Taxes attributable to such Lender (but only to the extent that the Borrower has not already indemnified the Administrative Agent for such Taxes and without limiting the obligation of the Borrower to do so), (ii) any Taxes attributable to such Lender\'s failure to comply with the provisions of Section 10.05 relating to the maintenance of a Participant Register, and (iii) any Excluded Taxes attributable to such Lender, in each case, that are payable or paid by the Administrative Agent in connection with any Loan Document, and any reasonable expenses arising therefrom or with respect thereto, whether or not such Taxes were correctly or legally imposed or asserted by the relevant Governmental Authority.

**(f) Lender Status Certificates.** Any Lender that is entitled to an exemption from or reduction of withholding Tax with respect to payments made under any Loan Document shall deliver to the Borrower and the Administrative Agent, at the time or times reasonably requested by the Borrower or the Administrative Agent, such properly completed and executed documentation reasonably requested by the Borrower or the Administrative Agent as will permit such payments to be made without withholding or at a reduced rate of withholding. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, each Lender shall, if reasonably requested by the Borrower or the Administrative Agent, deliver to the Borrower and the Administrative Agent executed copies of IRS Form W-9, W-8BEN, W-8BEN-E, W-8ECI, W-8IMY, or any other applicable form, certificate, or documentation prescribed by the Internal Revenue Service or reasonably requested to establish that such Lender is not subject to backup withholding or withholding under FATCA.

**(g) Defined Tax Terms.** For purposes of this Section 2.13, the term \"**Indemnified Taxes**\" means (i) Taxes, other than Excluded Taxes, imposed on or with respect to any payment made by or on account of any obligation of the Borrower under any Loan Document and (ii) to the extent not otherwise described in clause (i), Other Taxes. The term \"**Excluded Taxes**\" means, with respect to the Administrative Agent or any Lender, (A) Taxes imposed on or measured by its net income (however denominated), franchise Taxes, and branch profits Taxes, in each case (1) imposed as a result of such Administrative Agent or Lender being organized under the laws of, or having its principal office or applicable lending office located in, the jurisdiction imposing such Tax (or any political subdivision thereof), or (2) that are Other Connection Taxes, (B) U.S. federal withholding Taxes imposed on amounts payable to or for the account of such Administrative Agent or Lender with respect to an applicable interest in a Loan or Commitment pursuant to a law in effect on the date on which such Administrative Agent or Lender acquires such interest in the Loan or Commitment (other than pursuant to an assignment request by the Borrower under Section 10.05), except to the extent that such Administrative Agent\'s or Lender\'s assignor was entitled, at the time of assignment, to receive additional amounts from the Borrower with respect to such withholding Tax pursuant to this Section 2.13, (C) Taxes attributable to such Administrative Agent\'s or Lender\'s failure to comply with Section 2.13(f), and (D) any withholding Taxes imposed under FATCA. The term \"**Other Taxes**\" means any present or future stamp, court, documentary, intangible, filing, recording, excise, or property Taxes or similar Taxes arising from any payment made under any Loan Document or from the execution, delivery, performance, enforcement, or registration of, or otherwise with respect to, any Loan Document.

**(h) Increased Costs.** If any Change in Law shall:

> \(i\) impose, modify, or deem applicable any reserve, special deposit, liquidity, compulsory loan, insurance charge, or similar requirement against assets of, deposits with or for the account of, or credit extended or participated in by, any Lender or the Issuing Bank;
>
> \(ii\) subject any Lender or the Issuing Bank to any Taxes (other than (A) Indemnified Taxes, (B) Taxes described in clauses (A) through (D) of the definition of Excluded Taxes, and (C) Connection Income Taxes) on its loans, loan principal, letters of credit, commitments, or other obligations, or its deposits, reserves, other liabilities, or capital attributable thereto; or
>
> \(iii\) impose on any Lender or the Issuing Bank or the London interbank market or the U.S. syndicated loan market any other condition, cost, or expense affecting this Agreement or SOFR Loans made by such Lender or any Letter of Credit or participation therein;

and the result of any of the foregoing shall be to increase the cost to such Lender or the Issuing Bank of making, converting to, continuing, or maintaining any Loan (or of maintaining its obligation to make any Loan or its participation in any Letter of Credit), or to increase the cost to such Lender, the Issuing Bank, or the Administrative Agent of participating in, issuing, or maintaining any Letter of Credit, or to reduce the amount of any sum received or receivable by such Lender, the Issuing Bank, or the Administrative Agent hereunder (whether of principal, interest, or any other amount), then, upon request of such Lender, the Issuing Bank, or the Administrative Agent, the Borrower will pay to such Lender, the Issuing Bank, or the Administrative Agent, as the case may be, such additional amount or amounts as will compensate such Lender, the Issuing Bank, or the Administrative Agent for such additional costs incurred or reduction suffered.

**(i) Capital Requirements.** If any Lender or the Issuing Bank determines that any Change in Law affecting such Lender or the Issuing Bank or any lending office of such Lender or such Lender\'s or the Issuing Bank\'s holding company, if any, regarding capital or liquidity requirements has or would have the effect of reducing the rate of return on such Lender\'s or the Issuing Bank\'s capital or on the capital of such Lender\'s or the Issuing Bank\'s holding company, if any, as a consequence of this Agreement, the Commitments of such Lender, or the Loans made by, or participations in Letters of Credit held by, such Lender, or the Letters of Credit issued by the Issuing Bank, to a level below that which such Lender or the Issuing Bank or such Lender\'s or the Issuing Bank\'s holding company could have achieved but for such Change in Law (taking into consideration such Lender\'s or the Issuing Bank\'s policies and the policies of such Lender\'s or the Issuing Bank\'s holding company with respect to capital adequacy and liquidity), then from time to time the Borrower will pay to such Lender or the Issuing Bank, as the case may be, such additional amount or amounts as will compensate such Lender or the Issuing Bank or such Lender\'s or the Issuing Bank\'s holding company for any such reduction suffered.

**(j) Certificates; Delay in Requests.** A certificate of a Lender or the Issuing Bank setting forth in reasonable detail the amount or amounts necessary to compensate such Lender or the Issuing Bank or its holding company, as applicable, as specified in Section 2.13(h) or Section 2.13(i) and delivered to the Borrower shall be conclusive absent manifest error. The Borrower shall pay such Lender or the Issuing Bank the amount shown as due on any such certificate within 10 days after receipt thereof. Failure or delay on the part of any Lender or the Issuing Bank to demand compensation pursuant to this Section 2.13 shall not constitute a waiver of such Lender\'s or the Issuing Bank\'s right to demand such compensation; *provided* that the Borrower shall not be required to compensate a Lender or the Issuing Bank pursuant to this Section 2.13 for any increased costs or reductions incurred more than 180 days prior to the date that such Lender or the Issuing Bank, as the case may be, notifies the Borrower of the Change in Law giving rise to such increased costs or reductions and of such Lender\'s or the Issuing Bank\'s intention to claim compensation therefor, except that, if the Change in Law giving rise to such increased costs or reductions is retroactive, then the 180-day period referred to above shall be extended to include the period of retroactive effect thereof.

**(k) Illegality.** If any Lender determines that any Change in Law has made it unlawful, or that any Governmental Authority has asserted that it is unlawful, for any Lender or its applicable lending office to make, maintain, or fund SOFR Loans, or to determine or charge interest rates based upon SOFR or the Term SOFR Reference Rate, then, upon notice thereof by such Lender to the Borrower through the Administrative Agent, (i) any obligation of such Lender to make or continue SOFR Loans or to convert ABR Loans to SOFR Loans shall be suspended, and (ii) if such notice asserts the illegality of such Lender making or maintaining ABR Loans whose interest rate is determined by reference to the Term SOFR Reference Rate component of ABR, the interest rate on all affected ABR Loans of such Lender shall thereafter be determined by the Administrative Agent without reference to the Term SOFR Reference Rate component of ABR, in each case until such Lender notifies the Administrative Agent and the Borrower that the circumstances giving rise to such determination no longer exist. Upon receipt of such notice, (A) the Borrower shall, upon demand from such Lender (with a copy to the Administrative Agent), prepay or, if applicable, convert all SOFR Loans of such Lender to ABR Loans, either on the last day of the Interest Period therefor, if such Lender may lawfully continue to maintain such SOFR Loans to such day, or immediately, if such Lender may not lawfully continue to maintain such SOFR Loans, and (B) if such notice asserts the illegality of such Lender determining or charging interest rates based upon the Term SOFR Reference Rate component of ABR, the Administrative Agent shall during the period of such suspension compute the ABR applicable to such Lender without reference to the Term SOFR Reference Rate component thereof until the Administrative Agent is advised in writing by such Lender that it is no longer illegal for such Lender to determine or charge interest rates based upon the Term SOFR Reference Rate component of ABR. Upon any such prepayment or conversion, the Borrower shall also pay accrued interest on the amount so prepaid or converted.

**(l) Compensation for Losses.** In the event of (i) the payment of any principal of any SOFR Loan other than on the last day of an Interest Period applicable thereto (including as a result of an Event of Default), (ii) the conversion of any SOFR Loan other than on the last day of the Interest Period applicable thereto, (iii) the failure to borrow, convert, continue, or prepay any SOFR Loan on the date specified in any notice delivered pursuant hereto, or (iv) the assignment of any SOFR Loan other than on the last day of the Interest Period applicable thereto as a result of a request by the Borrower pursuant to Section 10.05, then, in any such event, the Borrower shall compensate each Lender for the loss, cost, and expense attributable thereto. In the case of a SOFR Loan, such loss, cost, or expense shall be deemed to include an amount determined by such Lender to be the excess, if any, of (A) the amount of interest that would have accrued on the principal amount of such SOFR Loan had such event not occurred, at the Adjusted Term SOFR that would have been applicable to such SOFR Loan, for the period from the date of such event to the last day of the then-current Interest Period therefor (or, in the case of a failure to borrow, convert, or continue, for the period that would have been the Interest Period for such SOFR Loan), over (B) the amount of interest that would accrue on such principal amount for such period at the interest rate that such Lender would bid were it to bid, at the commencement of such period, for Dollar deposits of a comparable amount and period from other banks in the U.S. syndicated loan market. A certificate of any Lender setting forth in reasonable detail any amount or amounts that such Lender is entitled to receive pursuant to this Section 2.13(l) shall be delivered to the Borrower and shall be conclusive absent manifest error.

**[Section 2.14 --- Extension of Maturity Date]{.underline}**

**(a) Extension Request.** The Borrower may, on one or more occasions, by written notice to the Administrative Agent (which shall promptly notify the Lenders), request an extension of the Maturity Date applicable to all or any portion of the Revolving Commitments and/or the Term Loans by not less than 12 months. Each such extension request shall specify the date on which the Borrower proposes that the extended Maturity Date shall occur and shall be delivered not less than 45 days and not more than 90 days prior to the then-existing Maturity Date to be extended.

**(b) Lender Elections.** Each Lender may, in its sole discretion, by written notice to the Administrative Agent given not later than the date specified by the Administrative Agent (which date shall be no earlier than 20 days after the Administrative Agent\'s notice of such extension request), agree to extend or decline to extend its applicable Commitments and/or Loans. Any Lender that fails to respond by such date shall be deemed to have declined such extension. No extension of the Maturity Date of any Lender\'s Commitments or Loans shall require the consent of any other Lender, and each extension shall be effective only as to those Lenders that affirmatively agree thereto.

**(c) Conditions to Effectiveness.** Any extension of a Maturity Date pursuant to this Section 2.14 shall become effective on the date specified in the applicable extension agreement, subject to satisfaction (or waiver by the extending Lenders and the Administrative Agent) of the following conditions precedent:

> \(i\) no Default or Event of Default shall have occurred and be continuing on the effective date of such extension or would result therefrom;
>
> \(ii\) the representations and warranties set forth in Article IV and in the other Loan Documents shall be true and correct in all material respects on and as of such effective date, except to the extent such representations and warranties specifically relate to an earlier date, in which case they shall be true and correct in all material respects as of such earlier date; and
>
> \(iii\) the Borrower shall have executed and delivered such agreements, instruments, and documents as the Administrative Agent may reasonably request to implement such extension.

**(d) Extended Classes.** If the Maturity Date of any Loans or Commitments is extended pursuant to this Section 2.14, such Loans or Commitments may, if the Administrative Agent so elects, be designated as a separate Class of Loans or Commitments for all purposes of this Agreement. Terms applicable solely to such extended Class, including maturity, pricing, commitment reductions, and amortization, may be established in the applicable extension agreement, so long as such terms do not adversely affect the rights of any non-extending Lenders other than by virtue of their election not to extend.

**(e) Latest First Lien Maturity Date.** Any extended maturity date established pursuant to this Section 2.14 shall become the new \"Latest First Lien Maturity Date\" for purposes of this Agreement and the Intercreditor Agreement to the extent such extended date is later than the then-current Latest First Lien Maturity Date.

**[ARTICLE III --- CONDITIONS PRECEDENT]{.underline}**

**[Section 3.01 --- Conditions to Closing Date]{.underline}**

The obligation of each Lender to make its Term Loan on the Closing Date, the obligation of each Revolving Lender to make Revolving Loans on the Closing Date, and the obligation of the Issuing Bank to issue any Letter of Credit on the Closing Date are subject to the satisfaction or waiver (in accordance with Section 10.02) of the following conditions precedent:

**(b) Opinions of Counsel.** The Administrative Agent shall have received a customary written opinion (including local counsel opinions where reasonably required by the Administrative Agent in connection with Mortgages or other perfection matters) of Kesswick, Tanaka & Moreau LLP, counsel to the Borrower, having offices at 51 West 52nd Street, 34th Floor, New York, NY 10019, addressed to the Administrative Agent and the Lenders and covering such matters relating to the Loan Parties, the Loan Documents, the perfection of security interests, and the Transactions as the Administrative Agent shall reasonably request.

**(c) Organizational Documents; Incumbency.** The Administrative Agent shall have received:

**(d) Officer\'s Certificates.** The Administrative Agent shall have received a certificate of a Responsible Officer of the Borrower, dated as of the Closing Date, certifying that (i) the conditions set forth in Sections 3.01(i), 3.01(j), and 3.01(k) have been satisfied, (ii) attached thereto are true and correct copies of the organizational and authorization documents described in Section 3.01(c), and (iii) after giving effect to the Transactions, the Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries, on a consolidated basis, are Solvent.

**(e) Security Interests; Collateral.** The Administrative Agent shall have received evidence reasonably satisfactory to it that the Security Documents create in favor of the Collateral Agent, for the benefit of the Secured Parties, a valid and perfected first-priority Lien (subject to Permitted Liens) on substantially all of the personal property Collateral of the Loan Parties required to be pledged on the Closing Date, including:

**(g) Fees and Expenses.** The Administrative Agent and the Lenders shall have received all fees and other amounts due and payable on or prior to the Closing Date, including all fees payable pursuant to the Fee Letter and all reasonable and documented out-of-pocket expenses of the Administrative Agent and its counsel, to the extent invoiced at least two Business Days prior to the Closing Date (except as otherwise agreed by the Borrower).

**[Section 3.02 --- Conditions to Each Revolving Borrowing and LC Issuance]{.underline}**

The obligation of each Revolving Lender to make any Revolving Loan after the Closing Date, and the obligation of the Issuing Bank to issue, amend, renew, or extend any Letter of Credit after the Closing Date, is subject to the satisfaction of the following conditions precedent:

**(a)** The Administrative Agent or the Issuing Bank, as applicable, shall have received a duly executed Borrowing Request, Interest Election Request, or application for issuance, amendment, renewal, or extension of a Letter of Credit, as applicable.

**(b)** The representations and warranties of the Borrower and each other Loan Party contained in Article IV and in each other Loan Document shall be true and correct in all material respects on and as of the date of such borrowing or issuance, amendment, renewal, or extension, except to the extent such representations and warranties expressly relate to an earlier date, in which case they shall be true and correct in all material respects as of such earlier date; *provided* that any representation and warranty qualified by materiality, Material Adverse Effect, or similar language shall be true and correct in all respects.

**(c)** No Default or Event of Default shall have occurred and be continuing, or would result from such borrowing or issuance, amendment, renewal, or extension.

**(d)** After giving effect to such borrowing or issuance, amendment, renewal, or extension, the Total Revolving Exposure shall not exceed the aggregate Revolving Commitments then in effect, and the aggregate LC Exposure shall not exceed the Letter of Credit Sub-Facility limit.

**(a)** contravene the terms of any of such Loan Party\'s Organizational Documents;

**(b)** conflict with or result in any breach or contravention of, or the creation of any Lien under, or require any payment to be made under, (i) any material Indebtedness of the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary, or (ii) any order, injunction, writ, or decree of any Governmental Authority or any arbitral award to which the Borrower or any Restricted Subsidiary or any of their respective property is subject; or

**(c)** violate any Applicable Law;

except, in the case of clauses (b) and (c), to the extent such conflict, breach, contravention, creation of Lien, payment, or violation would not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect.
